input.prompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm migrating my MSTest projects from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform.
My repo has a single Directory.Build.props at the root that is imported by
both production projects (e.g. `MyApp.csproj`) and test projects (e.g.
`MyApp.Tests.csproj`). I want to set `<OutputType>Exe</OutputType>` and the
MTP runner properties centrally, but I obviously don't want to turn my
library projects into executables. How do I write the condition in
Directory.Build.props so it only applies to the test projects?

data.content:
Use **`Directory.Build.targets`** (not `.props`) so the MSTest NuGet package has already set `$(IsTestProject)` by the time your condition is evaluated:

```xml
<!-- Directory.Build.targets -->
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup Condition="'$(IsTestProject)' == 'true'">
    <OutputType>Exe</OutputType>
    <EnableMSTestRunner>true</EnableMSTestRunner>
    <TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>true</TestingPlatformDotnetTestSupport>
  </PropertyGroup>
</Project>
```

**Why not `Directory.Build.props`?** It's imported *before* NuGet package `.props`, so `$(IsTestProject)` isn't set yet. `.targets` is imported after, so the property is available.

**Alternative** — if you must stay in `.props`, use a naming convention:

```xml
<PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
  ...
</PropertyGroup>
```

This is fragile if your test projects don't all follow the same suffix. The `.targets` + `IsTestProject` approach is the most robust.
data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about how to conditionally set MSBuild properties in a `Directory.Build.props` file so they only apply to test projects (not production/library projects) when migrating from VSTest to Microsoft.Testing.Platform (MTP).

This is a knowledge question about MSBuild conditions. Let me give a concise answer.

The standard approach is to use an MSBuild condition that checks for the presence of the MSTest package reference or a convention like the project name ending in `.Tests`. But since this is about MTP specifically, the best approach is to condition on `$(IsTestProject)` which is set by MSTest's SDK/NuGet props, or to use a naming convention.
 
The cleanest approach with MSTest 3.x+ is to use `$(IsTestProject)`, which the MSTest NuGet package sets automatically, or to condition on the project name ending with `.Tests`. However, since `Directory.Build.props` is imported very early in the build process before NuGet package props are loaded, `$(IsTestProject)` might not be available yet—so `Directory.Build.targets` would be the better place to put these conditional properties. Setting test project properties...

The most reliable approach is using `Directory.Build.targets` since the `IsTestProject` property from MSTest is available by that point, or falling back to a naming convention check in `Directory.Build.props` if needed. I'll go with the `Directory.Build.targets` solution as the primary recommendation since it's cleaner and more direct.
